strengths: 16+ years of engineering experience with demonstrated technical depth across distributed systems, microservices, and high-performance infrastructure (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP/second)
Current hands-on engagement with AI-agent infrastructure, MCP frameworks, and agentic workflows showing technical currency with emerging tooling
Proven ability to contribute across full technical stack with fluency in multiple languages and architectural patterns
concerns: No demonstrated executive or CTO-level leadership; all roles are Staff/Senior engineer positions with no evidence of building or scaling engineering teams to 30+ people or developing senior technical leadership layer

Limited production experience building and operating multi-agent AI systems in commercial contexts; agentic framework exposure is recent (2 months) and appears tooling/infrastructure-focused rather than core business-system application
Minimal fintech-specific experience; single short role at investment firm (Sep 2020–Dec 2021) without detail on regulatory compliance, credit systems, or financial risk management

key_factors.factor: Executive leadership and team scaling track record

response.justification: Szabo brings strong individual-contributor credentials with genuine depth in infrastructure and distributed systems. However, the CTO role explicitly requires demonstrated executive leadership—scaling teams, developing senior technical leaders, and managing hybrid engineering—none evident in his background. While he engages with agentic frameworks currently, his production experience with multi-agent AI systems in commercial contexts is limited to 2 months and appears tooling-focused. He has solid technical foundations but lacks both the leadership track record and proven agentic-AI operational experience the role demands.
